skill.name: error-diagnostics-error-analysis
skill.description: Guides systematic diagnosis of production errors, stack traces, logs, observability signals, and incident response workflows.
skill.summary: Diagnose application errors with structured root-cause analysis, observability checks, and prevention guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged code execution, network, environment, and weak-crypto patterns, but reviewed evidence shows these occur in Markdown examples and diagnostic playbooks rather than executable skill code. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration intent, or malicious automation was found. Residual risk is low because the skill handles logs, traces, and production incident data that may contain secrets or PII.

low_findings.title: Static Code-Execution Hits Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported shell and dynamic import patterns appear inside Markdown instructions and fenced examples, not executable skill files. The Go import block and inline backticks explain diagnostic workflows and resource links rather than launching commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is in Markdown prose or code fences, and no executable script entrypoint was found in the scanned files. The static labels match syntax used for documentation, not runtime behavior.

low_findings.title: Network and Environment Access Are Observability Samples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE with low residual risk. The network calls, URLs, and environment variables are sample integrations for axios, fetch, Sentry, runbooks, dashboards, and status pages. They do not show unauthorized data transfer, but pasted examples should be reviewed before production use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context names standard observability tools and placeholder endpoints. No evidence shows secret collection or exfiltration beyond normal telemetry examples.

low_findings.title: Diagnostics May Include Sensitive Incident Data
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for a minor operational risk. The skill is designed to analyze logs, traces, production incidents, user impact, and error context. It includes redaction guidance, but users must still avoid exposing secrets, PII, tokens, and production-only details to AI tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly targets logs and production incidents, which often contain sensitive values. Confidence is high for data-handling risk but not higher because the skill also instructs users to redact secrets and PII.

content.value_statement: Production errors often span logs, traces, services, and recent deployments. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for root-cause analysis and prevention.
actual_capabilities: Classifies runtime, integration, performance, configuration, and security errors by severity and observability.
Guides root-cause analysis using reproduction steps, call-chain review, recent changes, and hypothesis testing.
Explains stack trace, structured log, correlation ID, and distributed tracing analysis patterns.
Provides observability examples for Sentry, DataDog APM, OpenTelemetry, alerts, and runbooks.
Suggests fixes, tests, monitoring improvements, and preventive measures for recurring errors.
Includes incident communication templates and deliverable checklists for production response.
limitations: It does not execute diagnostics or access monitoring systems by itself.
It depends on accurate logs, traces, stack traces, metrics, and deployment context from the user.
Sample code must be reviewed and adapted before use in production systems.
Sensitive logs, secrets, tokens, and PII should be removed before sharing context.

use_cases.title: Triage a Production Incident
use_cases.description: Build a timeline, identify affected services, evaluate impact, and plan safe mitigation steps.

use_cases.title: Debug a Failing Service
use_cases.description: Analyze stack traces, logs, correlation IDs, and recent changes to find the most likely root cause.

use_cases.title: Improve Error Observability
use_cases.description: Design better structured logging, tracing, alerting, and runbooks after recurring failures.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze an Error Message
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for one visible error.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this error message and explain the likely cause, missing context, and safest next debugging steps: [paste redacted error].

prompt_templates.title: Review Logs and Stack Trace
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused debugging with logs and code context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this stack trace, log excerpt, and recent change summary to identify likely root causes and verification steps: [paste redacted details].

prompt_templates.title: Build Incident Timeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an incident timeline from these metrics, alerts, traces, and deployment events. Include impact, hypotheses, evidence, and mitigation options.

prompt_templates.title: Design Prevention Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this root-cause analysis into a prevention plan with code fixes, tests, alerts, dashboards, runbook steps, and ownership recommendations.

output_examples.input: A payment service returns intermittent timeout errors after a deployment.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a prioritized hypothesis list, evidence to collect, rollback criteria, database pool checks, and follow-up tests.

output_examples.input: A stack trace points to a null value in an asynchronous request handler.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies likely invalid state creation points, asks for caller context, and suggests validation, reproduction, and regression tests.

output_examples.input: Error alerts are noisy and do not identify affected users.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends fingerprints, correlation IDs, user impact fields, alert thresholds, and runbook updates.

best_practices: Provide redacted stack traces, timestamps, service names, deployment history, and correlation IDs together.
Separate confirmed facts from hypotheses so the analysis can rank evidence clearly.
Ask for verification steps, tests, rollback criteria, and monitoring changes with every fix.
anti_patterns: Do not paste secrets, access tokens, customer PII, or complete production dumps into prompts.
Do not apply production changes without approval, rollback plans, and impact checks.
Do not treat a single stack trace as proof when logs, metrics, and recent changes disagree.

faq.question: Can this skill access my logs automatically?
faq.answer: No. It only analyzes the context you provide or the files an enabled AI tool can access.

faq.question: Is this only for production incidents?
faq.answer: No. It also helps with local failures, staging regressions, flaky behavior, and recurring application errors.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide redacted errors, stack traces, timestamps, service names, recent changes, metrics, and reproduction steps.

faq.question: Does it write code fixes?
faq.answer: It can propose fixes and tests, but implementation depends on the surrounding repository and user approval.

faq.question: Can it help with observability design?
faq.answer: Yes. The playbook covers structured logging, correlation IDs, Sentry, DataDog, OpenTelemetry, alerts, and runbooks.

faq.question: How should I handle sensitive data?
faq.answer: Redact secrets, tokens, customer PII, session values, and production-only identifiers before sharing diagnostic context.
